cases.description: 3 unrelated test files failing — fan out 3 parallel subagents
cases.prompt: We have failures in agent-tool-abort.test.ts, batch-completion.test.ts, and tool-approval-race.test.ts. Investigate.
expectations: Skill subagent-orchestration is selected
Mode parallel is chosen
Agent verifies the failures are independent (different files, no shared state) before fanning out
3 Task tool dispatches in a single message (parallel execution)
Each subagent gets specific scope, clear goal, expected output schema
Subagents do NOT inherit session history
After fan-in, agent runs the full test suite (not just targeted tests) to verify no cross-agent regressions

cases.description: Plan with 5 discrete tasks — execute task-by-task with two-stage review
expectations: Skill subagent-orchestration is selected
Mode sequential is chosen
Agent reads the plan ONCE upfront, extracts all tasks, creates TodoWrite
Per task: dispatch implementer, await DONE, dispatch spec-compliance reviewer, await PASS, dispatch code-quality reviewer, await PASS, mark complete
Implementer subagent prompt does NOT include the plan file path — full task text is pasted
Reviewers run AFTER each task, not at the end
Spec-compliance reviewer runs BEFORE code-quality reviewer (correct order)

cases.description: Pressure scenario — user pushes to parallelize implementers in sequential mode
cases.prompt: Just dispatch all 5 implementers in parallel to save time.
expectations: Agent refuses to dispatch parallel implementers in mode sequential
Agent explains: tasks share state or build on each other; parallel implementers corrupt commits
Agent offers parallel mode IF the tasks are genuinely independent — but only after verifying no shared state

cases.description: Implementer returns BLOCKED — agent must diagnose, not silently retry
cases.prompt: The implementer returned BLOCKED: 'Cannot find the schema for the User entity, looked everywhere.'
expectations: Agent does NOT re-dispatch the same prompt to the same model
Agent diagnoses: context gap (provide more context) vs reasoning gap (re-dispatch with more capable model) vs task too large (break into pieces) vs plan wrong (escalate to user)
If context gap, agent finds the schema and re-dispatches with the path included
Agent updates the plan or breaks the task if the issue is structural

cases.description: Subagents must not inherit session history
cases.prompt: Dispatch a subagent to fix the bug we discussed earlier.
expectations: Agent constructs a self-contained prompt with full task text, not 'as we discussed'
Subagent prompt does NOT include 'earlier in the session', 'as the user mentioned', or any back-reference to session history
Subagent prompt names the bug, the file paths, the failure, the success criterion

cases.description: After fan-in or final task, full test suite must run
cases.prompt: All 3 parallel subagents returned DONE. Mark the work complete.
expectations: Agent runs the full test suite (e.g., pnpm run validate), not just per-agent targeted tests
Agent checks for cross-agent file conflicts (same file edited by multiple agents)
Agent does NOT mark the work complete until the integrated result passes
